summary: Excellent classic‑74 soundboard capture highlighted by a legendary Eyes, an epic Weather Report Suite > Let It Grow and a soaring Morning Dew encore.
review: The June‑18‑1974 show is hailed as one of the band’s most iconic performances – reviewers rave about a monster ‘Eyes of the World’, a blistering Weather Report Suite into Let It Grow, and an unforgettable Morning Dew encore. The pitch‑fixed soundboard source sounds crisp, balanced and even slightly better than the famed Charlie Miller SBD, making the intricate layers of guitar, keyboards and rhythm section shine.

ai_rating.rationale: All nine reviews give five‑star ratings and uniformly praise both the musical performance (standout jams, legendary songs) and the soundboard recording quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Classic 1974 board with a crisp, well‑balanced mix; reviewers note “hammering” keyboards, “perfect” drum beat and a slight mids‑softening upgrade over the Charlie Miller SBD.

standout_songs: Weather Report Suite > Let It Grow
show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers describe the concert as a cosmic, harmonic journey with seamless transitions—especially the LIG → The Other One jam and an emotionally charged second set.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for his soaring lead guitar tone – “perfect” and doing what the Dead do best.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass described as “bumping flambantly,” adding depth and drive to the jams.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned alongside Jerry as delivering quintessential rhythm work that fuels the band’s energy.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s keyboards are called “hammering,” providing rich, layered texture throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill’s drumming is noted as “beating perfectly,” anchoring the complex improvisations.
